title: ShopEKS Revival — Phase D Capstone
scenario:
🔥 ShopEKS is down. Four independent failures have stacked up:
1. ArgoCD `targetRevision` points to a deleted branch `feature/old-checkout` — change it to `main`.
2. The `checkout` PDB has `minAvailable: 3` but only 3 replicas exist — drain is blocked forever.
3. The Karpenter `prod-pool` NodePool uses `WhenEmptyOrUnderutilized` consolidation, which is evicting pods every 5 minutes under load.
4. The `orders` Deployment pulls from ECR with the wrong role annotation — it references `arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/wrong-role` instead of `arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/orders-ecr-pull`.
Fix all four issues.

data.application.yaml:
targetRevision: feature/old-checkout
repoURL: https://github.com/shopeks/platform
path: k8s/overlays/prod

starterYaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: argocd-app-config
  namespace: argocd
data:
  application.yaml: |
    targetRevision: feature/old-checkout
    repoURL: https://github.com/shopeks/platform
    path: k8s/overlays/prod
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: checkout-pdb
  namespace: default
spec:
  minAvailable: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: checkout
---
apiVersion: karpenter.sh/v1
kind: NodePool
metadata:
  name: prod-pool
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      requirements:
        - key: karpenter.sh/capacity-type
          operator: In
          values: ["on-demand"]
      nodeClassRef:
        group: karpenter.k8s.aws
        kind: EC2NodeClass
        name: default
  disruption:
    consolidationPolicy: WhenEmptyOrUnderutilized
    consolidateAfter: 5m
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: orders-sa
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/wrong-role

hints.text:
Fix 1: In the ArgoCD ConfigMap, change `targetRevision: feature/old-checkout` to `targetRevision: main`.
Fix 2: The checkout PDB has 3 replicas and `minAvailable: 3` — drain can never evict any pod. Change to `minAvailable: 2`.

Fix 3: `WhenEmptyOrUnderutilized` consolidation tries to bin-pack nodes under load, causing pod churn. Change `consolidationPolicy` to `WhenEmpty` and increase `consolidateAfter` to `30m`.
Fix 4: In the ServiceAccount, change the role ARN to `arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/orders-ecr-pull`.

All four fixes at a glance:
1. ConfigMap: `targetRevision: main`
2. PDB: `minAvailable: 2`
3. NodePool: `consolidationPolicy: WhenEmpty`, `consolidateAfter: 30m`
4. ServiceAccount annotation: `arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/orders-ecr-pull`

solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: argocd-app-config
  namespace: argocd
data:
  application.yaml: |
    targetRevision: main
    repoURL: https://github.com/shopeks/platform
    path: k8s/overlays/prod
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: checkout-pdb
  namespace: default
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: checkout
---
apiVersion: karpenter.sh/v1
kind: NodePool
metadata:
  name: prod-pool
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      requirements:
        - key: karpenter.sh/capacity-type
          operator: In
          values: ["on-demand"]
      nodeClassRef:
        group: karpenter.k8s.aws
        kind: EC2NodeClass
        name: default
  disruption:
    consolidationPolicy: WhenEmpty
    consolidateAfter: 30m
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: orders-sa
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/orders-ecr-pull
